sections.paragraph: according to דֶּהָוֵא כתיב m. pl. Ch. Dahi, [Dehavites], pr.n. of a people from which a colony was brought to Samaria, Ezr 4:9. They seem to have been the Δάοι, Herod. i. 125 (prob. villagers from Pers. ده deh, dih, a village), a Persian tribe [near the Caspian sea, Strab. xi. p. 480, Plin. H. N. xi. 17], of which a farther account is given in Lorsbach, Archiv. ii. p. 274. Mention is also made of this people in the Zendavesta.
